title: Task Ledger Drift
description: Interleaved task ownership with stale pings and un-mentioned duties. The subject must answer status probes strictly by who currently holds the task per human:owner's assignment posts, not by @-mentions, while the ledger flips three times across ~230 messages: T1 departs to a peer and returns via an indirect 'previous holder resumes' announcement that back-references an assignment ~120 buried messages earlier, and T3 is assigned in a single owner post sandwiched between two filler blocks and only probed ~190 messages later. Lexically identical 'status on T1?' probes flip their correct action three times, including an inverted-cue probe where the subject must speak without being mentioned. Side traps: a peer posts a perfect verbatim-copyable status of the subject's task seconds before the subject is asked for it, and a respond-marked arbitration event is fenced by word-count, agreement-filler, and fence-sitting noise gates.
